text: What if saving one species could protect countless others? Meet umbrella species—organisms whose conservation can prevent the decline of many other species. These species serve as a shield, protecting entire ecosystems from collapse. Think of the tiger. Its protection means safeguarding the entire forest it roams. But here's the catch: balancing conservation efforts is complex and uncertain. Not every umbrella species can carry the weight. Some may fail, leading to unexpected outcomes. Curious about how we can help? Explore conservation efforts around you!
